sourceMovie.title: The Graduate
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Graduate works as a drama movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The drama and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Lobster
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind The Lobster (2015) clearly understood what makes drama work — the same quality that keeps The Graduate rewatchable. Expect the same funny rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

recommendations.title: Café Society
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Café Society (2016) is the kind of film The Graduate fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The drama overlap is real, but it's the funny emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

recommendations.title: Licorice Pizza
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Licorice Pizza shares The Graduate's gift for drama — the 2021 film delivers romance without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

recommendations.title: The Mirror Has Two Faces
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Much of what makes The Graduate compelling — the drama, the romance — shows up in The Mirror Has Two Faces (1996) in a slightly different form. Both films understand that funny storytelling works best when it trusts the audience.

recommendations.title: It Ends with Us
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The drama energy of The Graduate is alive in It Ends with Us (2024), filtered through a different story but the same emotional register. It earns its place on this list with a bittersweet payoff that rivals the original pick.

recommendations.title: Materialists
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Materialists (2025) operates in the same drama as The Graduate, but with its own distinct voice and pacing. The bittersweet payoff will feel satisfyingly familiar without retreading the same ground.

recommendations.title: Last Year at Marienbad
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Last Year at Marienbad (1961) belongs to the same conversation as The Graduate — both anchor their drama in romance emotion that doesn't let go. The bittersweet undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

recommendations.title: Corpse Bride
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Graduate's romance stayed with you, Corpse Bride (2005) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The fantasy through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: Changeling
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where The Graduate excels in drama, Changeling (2008) doubles down on crime to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: Roma
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Roma (2018) is the natural next step after The Graduate: same drama DNA, different perspective. The tense streak and craft elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Graduate?
faq.answer: Try The Lobster, Café Society, Licorice Pizza—each shares DNA with The Graduate while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Graduate?
faq.answer: The Graduate (1967) is best described as Drama, Romance, Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Graduate worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
